title: Integration: incremental evaluator pipeline
analysis:
This task integrates all M2 incremental evaluation components (Snapshot, EvaluationGraph, CacheStore, dependency tracking, dirty/demand cones) into the Engine, replacing the M1 simple evaluator while maintaining full backward compatibility.

## Current State

Engine (lib.rs) has four evaluation methods:
- eval(): Two-pass stateless evaluation (params first, then let bindings)
- eval_cached(): Version-based caching with early cutoff
- check(): Calls eval() then runs ConstraintChecker
- build(): Calls check() then runs GeometryKernel

Engine holds: constraint_checker, geometry_kernel, cache (CacheStore), param_overrides.

All M2 incremental components are fully implemented in separate modules:
- snapshot.rs: Snapshot with provenance, O(1) clone via PersistentMap
- graph.rs: EvaluationGraph with typed nodes (ValueCellNode, ConstraintNodeData, RealizationNodeData)
- deps.rs: DependencyTrace, ReverseDependencyIndex, build_trace_map()
- demand.rs: DemandRegistry with always_demanded + demand_cone
- dirty.rs: compute_dirty_cone(), compute_eval_set(), topological_sort()
- cache.rs: CacheStore with record_evaluation (early cutoff), dirty_reasons, freshness

## Integration Approach

### Phase 1: Refactor Engine internals
Add Snapshot + dependency structures as Engine fields. eval() builds Snapshot from CompiledModule, evaluates all values (same results), stores internal state. check()/build() continue to work identically. The Snapshot stores evaluated values with determinacy state. eval_cached() and set_param_and_invalidate() remain untouched for backward compat.

### Phase 2: Add edit_param()
New method creates child snapshot with Edit provenance, computes dirty cone from changed cells, intersects with demand cone, evaluates only the intersection in topological order. Returns EvalResult with updated values.

### Phase 3: Early cutoff in incremental path
When re-evaluating a node produces an unchanged content hash, remove its downstream dependents from the remaining eval set. This prevents unnecessary re-computation.

### Phase 4: Freshness propagation
Dirty nodes transition to Pending before re-evaluation, then back to Final. Non-dirty nodes remain Final.

## Bracket Fixture Analysis
- 5 params: width(80mm), height(100mm), thickness(5mm), fillet_radius(3mm), hole_diameter(6mm)
- 1 let: volume = width * height * thickness (reads: width, height, thickness)
- 3 constraints: C0(thickness > 2mm), C1(thickness < width/4), C2(hole_diameter < thickness*2)
- 0 realizations in the test fixture

When width changes: dirty_cone = {volume, C1}. With all constraints demanded: eval_set = {C1} (volume dirty but not demanded). With all values+constraints demanded: eval_set = {volume, C1}.

When fillet_radius changes: dirty_cone = {} (nothing reads fillet_radius).

## Key Constraint: OCCT Thread Safety
Geometry evaluation remains sequential. e2e_bracket test still requires --test-threads=1. No concurrent kernel access introduced.

prerequisites.description: Add `mark_pending()` method to CacheStore in cache.rs for freshness state transitions during incremental evaluation. This sets a node's freshness to Pending{last_substantive: Some(result_hash)} before re-evaluation.

steps.description: Write canary backward-compat test `cold_start_eval_produces_correct_values` in tests/incremental.rs. Creates Engine with MockConstraintChecker, evals bracket_compiled_module(), verifies all 6 values (width=80mm, height=100mm, thickness=5mm, fillet_radius=3mm, hole_diameter=6mm, volume=4e-5 m³). This test must pass before AND after the Engine refactoring.

Refactor Engine to hold Snapshot and dependency structures. Add fields: current_snapshot: Option<Snapshot>, reverse_index: Option<ReverseDependencyIndex>, trace_map: Option<HashMap<NodeId, DependencyTrace>>, demand: DemandRegistry, next_snapshot_id: u64, next_version_id: u64, last_eval_set: Vec<NodeId>. Modify eval() to: (1) build Snapshot from CompiledModule, (2) build EvaluationGraph and dependency structures, (3) demand all ValueCells via DemandRegistry, (4) evaluate params then let bindings (same two-pass logic), (5) update snapshot values with evaluated results, (6) record evaluations in CacheStore, (7) store snapshot+deps in Engine. check()/build() unchanged externally. Add last_eval_set() accessor and snapshot() accessor. All 122 M1 tests must pass.

Write test `edit_param_returns_updated_values` in tests/incremental.rs. Eval bracket module cold-start, then call engine.edit_param(width_id, Value::length(0.1)) [100mm]. Verify: width=100mm, volume changed to 0.1*0.1*0.005=5e-5 m³, height/thickness/fillet_radius/hole_diameter unchanged from initial eval.

Implement basic edit_param() method on Engine. Signature: pub fn edit_param(&mut self, cell: ValueCellId, new_value: Value) -> EvalResult. Implementation: (1) panic if no current_snapshot (must call eval first), (2) clone current snapshot, (3) update the changed cell's value in snapshot, (4) rebuild ValueMap from snapshot values + the new param value, (5) re-evaluate all let bindings that depend on changed cells (for now: re-evaluate ALL let bindings to keep it simple), (6) return EvalResult with updated values. This passes step-3's test.

Write test `edit_param_snapshot_provenance` in tests/incremental.rs. After eval(), verify engine.snapshot().provenance == SnapshotProvenance::Initial. After edit_param(width, 100mm), verify engine.snapshot().provenance == SnapshotProvenance::Edit { changed: {width_id}, parent: SnapshotId(0) }. Also verify snapshot IDs increment (initial=0, after edit=1).

Set provenance correctly in eval() and edit_param(). In eval(): set snapshot provenance to SnapshotProvenance::Initial, id=SnapshotId(counter), version=VersionId(counter). In edit_param(): increment counters, create child snapshot with SnapshotProvenance::Edit { changed: HashSet containing the edited cell, parent: previous snapshot id }.

Write test `edit_param_partial_reeval_only_dirty_demanded` in tests/incremental.rs. Eval bracket module cold-start with all constraints demanded (via check-like demand setup). Edit width from 80mm to 100mm. Verify via engine.last_eval_set(): (1) NodeId::Value(volume_id) IS in eval set (volume reads width), (2) NodeId::Constraint(C1) IS in eval set (C1 reads width and thickness), (3) NodeId::Value(fillet_radius_id) NOT in eval set, (4) NodeId::Value(hole_diameter_id) NOT in eval set, (5) NodeId::Constraint(C0) NOT in eval set (C0 only reads thickness), (6) NodeId::Constraint(C2) NOT in eval set (C2 reads hole_diameter and thickness, not width).

Wire dirty/demand cones into edit_param(). Modify eval() to also demand all ConstraintNodes and RealizationNodes (matching check/build scope). In edit_param(): (1) compute changed set from the edited cell, (2) call compute_dirty_cone() with changed set and reverse_index, (3) call compute_eval_set() to intersect dirty with demand and topo-sort, (4) iterate eval set: for Value nodes, evaluate expression with current values; for Constraint/Realization nodes, record in trace but skip evaluation (deferred to check/build), (5) record evaluations in CacheStore, (6) store eval set in self.last_eval_set, (7) update current_snapshot with new values. Only re-evaluate Value nodes that are in the eval set.

Write test `content_hash_early_cutoff_prevents_downstream_eval` in tests/incremental.rs. Build custom module via TopologyTemplateBuilder: param a (Real, default 5.0), let x = a - a (always 0.0 regardless of a), let y = x + 1.0 (reads x). Eval cold-start. Edit a from 5.0 to 7.0. Verify: (1) NodeId::Value(x_id) IS in eval set (x reads a, so x is dirty), (2) NodeId::Value(y_id) NOT in eval set (x re-evaluated but produced same hash 0.0, early cutoff removes y), (3) y's value is still 1.0 from cache.

Apply content-hash early cutoff in edit_param's incremental evaluation loop. When evaluating a Value node: (1) evaluate expression, (2) call cache.record_evaluation() which returns EvalOutcome::Changed or Unchanged, (3) if Unchanged (early cutoff): remove this node's downstream dependents from the remaining eval set using the reverse_index — specifically, for each dependent in reverse_index.dependents_of(vcid), remove it from the eval set if it's present. Track cutoff nodes separately for the eval trace.

Write test `freshness_final_after_cold_start` in tests/incremental.rs. After eval() cold start on bracket module, verify all value cell nodes in cache have Freshness::Final. Write test `freshness_transitions_during_edit` in same file: after eval(), edit width. Verify: (1) before edit_param evaluates, dirty nodes would be Pending (verify by checking cache state), (2) after edit_param returns, all re-evaluated nodes are back to Freshness::Final, (3) nodes not in eval set remain Freshness::Final throughout.

Ensure freshness propagation in eval() and edit_param(). In eval() cold start: record_evaluation already sets Final. In edit_param(): before the evaluation loop, call cache.mark_pending() on all nodes in the eval set (transitions them to Pending with last_substantive hash). During evaluation loop: record_evaluation sets them back to Final. After loop: verify non-evaluated cached nodes retain Final. This ensures the full lifecycle: Final → Pending → Final for re-evaluated nodes.

design_decisions.decision: edit_param() returns EvalResult (values only), not CheckResult or a richer type
design_decisions.rationale: Matches the task specification. Constraint re-evaluation is deferred to check(). The eval trace (last_eval_set) includes constraint NodeIds for verification that they WOULD be re-evaluated, but actual constraint checker invocation happens in check().

design_decisions.decision: Engine exposes last_eval_set() and snapshot() accessors for testing
design_decisions.rationale: The task requires verifying which nodes were actually evaluated during edit_param. last_eval_set() returns the Vec<NodeId> of the dirty∩demand intersection. snapshot() returns &Snapshot for provenance verification. Both are pub methods on Engine, useful for debugging beyond just tests.

design_decisions.decision: eval() always does a cold start (builds new Snapshot), edit_param() does incremental from last eval
design_decisions.rationale: Simplest correct approach. eval() is the 'reset' that establishes baseline state. edit_param() is the incremental path. If someone calls eval() again, it rebuilds from scratch. This avoids complex topology-change detection and keeps backward compat trivial.

design_decisions.decision: eval_cached() and set_param_and_invalidate() remain unchanged for backward compatibility
design_decisions.rationale: These methods are used by existing tests (cache.rs tests). They implement version-based caching which is orthogonal to the Snapshot-based incremental approach. Removing them would break existing tests. They can be deprecated in a future task.

design_decisions.decision: eval() demands all ValueCells, Constraints, and Realizations (full demand set)
design_decisions.rationale: For cold start, everything needs evaluation. The demand registry is populated eagerly so that subsequent edit_param() calls can immediately compute the correct eval set without the caller needing to specify what to demand.

design_decisions.decision: Evaluation loop in edit_param() is inline, not delegated to a Scheduler abstraction
design_decisions.rationale: The eval set from compute_eval_set() is already topologically sorted. The evaluation loop iterates it sequentially, evaluating Value nodes via reify_expr::eval_expr. No need for a formal Scheduler trait since geometry evaluation remains sequential (OCCT thread safety). A Scheduler abstraction can be introduced later when concurrent evaluation is needed.

design_decisions.decision: Early cutoff in edit_param() dynamically shrinks the eval set during iteration
design_decisions.rationale: When a re-evaluated Value node produces the same content hash, its downstream dependents are removed from the remaining eval set. This uses the reverse_index to find dependents and filters the remaining iteration. More efficient than re-running compute_eval_set().

design_decisions.decision: Constraint and Realization nodes in the eval set are tracked but not evaluated by edit_param()
design_decisions.rationale: edit_param() returns EvalResult (values only). Constraint evaluation requires the ConstraintChecker trait (batch API). Realization evaluation requires the GeometryKernel. These are evaluated when check()/build() are called. The eval set still includes these node types so that last_eval_set() accurately reflects what WOULD need re-evaluation.

reuse.how: Used in eval() to create the initial snapshot from a CompiledModule. Builds the EvaluationGraph, computes topology fingerprint, initializes all values to (Undef, Undetermined).
Called internally by Snapshot::from_compiled_module(). Converts TopologyTemplates into typed graph nodes backed by PersistentMap.
Built in eval() after creating the Snapshot. Maps ValueCellId → Set<NodeId> for forward propagation in dirty cone computation.
Built in eval() alongside reverse_index. Provides forward dependency traces (NodeId → DependencyTrace) for topological sort and demand cone computation.
Created in eval(), populated with all ValueCells/Constraints/Realizations, then rebuild_cone() computes the full demand cone via backward BFS. Persists across edit_param() calls.
Called in edit_param() with the set of changed cells and the reverse_index. Returns the set of all nodes transitively affected by the change.
Called in edit_param() to intersect dirty cone with demand cone and topologically sort the result. Returns Vec<NodeId> in evaluation order.
Called during both cold-start and incremental evaluation to record node results. Returns EvalOutcome::Changed/Unchanged for early cutoff detection.
Used during evaluation to extract the dependency trace from compiled expressions, passed to CacheStore::record_evaluation().
Core expression evaluator, called for each Value node during both cold-start and incremental evaluation. Takes a CompiledExpr and ValueMap, returns Value.

Used extensively in tests/incremental.rs. bracket_compiled_module() provides the standard bracket fixture. TopologyTemplateBuilder builds custom test modules for early cutoff tests. MockConstraintChecker provides predetermined constraint results.
edit_param() clones the current snapshot to create a child. O(1) structural sharing means no copying of the graph or values map until mutation.
